title: Cheap apartments for rent in Euclid, OH
metaTitle: Cheap Apartments for Rent in Euclid, OH
metaDescription: Looking for cheap apartments for rent in Euclid, Ohio? Compare Cleveland-area rent benchmarks and Section 8 facts, then book a free showing nearby.
primaryKeyword: cheap apartments for rent Euclid ohio
answerBox: Euclid, Ohio is a Lake Erie suburb directly northeast of Cleveland, bordering the city's Collinwood-Nottingham and Euclid-Green neighborhoods. Greater Cleveland apartment rents run roughly $1,060–$1,350/month by size per Zumper (July 2026). Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't manage units inside Euclid but manages Section 8-friendly homes just over the line in Cleveland's Collinwood area (ZIP 44110), our second-largest ZIP by unit count.

body: Euclid sits directly northeast of Cleveland on the Lake Erie shoreline, one of Cuyahoga County's larger lakefront suburbs. Its rental stock includes older apartment buildings along the lakefront corridor and a mix of two-family homes on residential side streets.

There's no independently published Euclid-only rent index, so the closest reliable benchmark for comparing prices is the Greater Cleveland metro-area data below. Actual Euclid pricing depends on the building, its distance from the lake, and its condition.

sections.h2: How much do apartments cost near Euclid?
body: Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the Greater Cleveland median asking rent at $1,250/month overall, broken down by size below. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment communities, shows higher averages for the same bedroom counts. Both sources confirm Cleveland-area rents run well below the U.S. national median.

sections.h2: Euclid's location and nearby Cleveland neighborhoods
body: Euclid directly borders Cleveland's **Euclid-Green** neighborhood, the city's far-northeast corner, and sits just north of **Collinwood-Nottingham**, both along the same stretch of Lake Erie shoreline. Nearby North Shore Collinwood, home to the Waterloo Arts District, shares the same lakefront setting.
GCRTA runs bus service through Euclid connecting to Downtown Cleveland and the Tower City rail hub, where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront lines converge for onward transit.

items: **Nearby landmark:** Waterloo Arts District (North Shore Collinwood)
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes to Downtown/Tower City

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have apartments in Euclid?
body: We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, concentrated on the East and Southeast side — including **Collinwood/Nottingham (ZIP 44110)**, our second-largest ZIP by unit count, which directly borders Euclid — but we don't currently have listings inside Euclid itself.

If you're open to that adjacent Cleveland neighborhood, [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and tell our team your bedroom count and budget. You can also browse our [Cleveland apartments guide](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or the [Euclid apartment listings roundup](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/apartments-for-rent-euclid-ohio/) for other nearby options.

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly apartments near Euclid
body: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, so whether a landlord accepts a Housing Choice Voucher depends on local ordinance or individual policy. As of July 2026, the reported Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local voucher-holder protection ordinance are Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. Note that Euclid and South Euclid are two separate cities — Euclid itself is not currently reported on that protected list, so acceptance there depends on the individual landlord; renters should verify directly.

Every rental home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including our homes near Euclid in Collinwood. Vouchers in Cuyahoga County are administered by the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)**; see our [Section 8 apartments near Euclid guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/section-8-apartments-for-rent-euclid-ohio/) for details on applying and waitlists.

sections.h2: Tips for finding a cheap apartment near Euclid
body: When comparing prices, check both Zumper's median-asking figures and RentCafe's professionally-managed averages — the gap between the two mostly reflects older lakefront buildings versus newer managed complexes, not a pricing error. Widening your search a few blocks south into Cleveland's Collinwood-Nottingham neighborhood can surface more voucher-friendly options along the same Lake Erie corridor.

If a Housing Choice Voucher is part of your plan, start the CMHA application alongside your apartment search — preliminary intake runs year-round with no closing date, but placement is by random lottery and can take anywhere from a few days to several months.

faq.q: Are there cheap apartments for rent in Euclid, Ohio?
faq.a: Euclid's rental stock includes older lakefront apartment buildings and two-family homes on residential streets. There's no Euclid-only rent index, but Greater Cleveland metro rents run roughly $1,060–$1,350/month by size per Zumper's July 2026 report; exact Euclid pricing depends on the building.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage apartments in Euclid?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ managed rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's East and Southeast side, including Collinwood/Nottingham (ZIP 44110) — our second-largest ZIP by unit count — which directly borders Euclid. Book a free showing to see what's currently available nearby.

faq.q: Does Euclid accept Section 8 housing vouchers?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers. Euclid is a separate city from South Euclid, which is reportedly on the short list of Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local voucher-protection ordinance; Euclid itself is not currently reported on that list, so acceptance depends on the individual landlord.
Euclid borders Cleveland's Euclid-Green neighborhood, the city's far-northeast corner, and Collinwood-Nottingham (ZIP 44110) just to the south, all sharing the same Lake Erie shoreline.

faq.q: How do I get from Euclid to Downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: GCRTA bus routes connect Euclid to Downtown Cleveland and the Tower City rail hub, where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines converge for onward travel around the region.
